title: Journey Mode
text: Journey Mode is a difficulty option that can be selected during character and world creation. It provides the player access to a wide array of customization options during gameplay, including duplication of items, modification of time and weather , and difficulty sliders. Journey Mode is only accessible with Journey characters in Journey worlds (Journey worlds can only be accessed using Journey characters, and Journey characters can only play in Journey worlds). Journey Mode is intended to be Terraria 's "creative" or "tourist" mode, making it relatively easy to acquire items and explore the game's various elements and mechanics without having to overcome its normal progression of challenges; though Journey players do have the ability to adjust the game's difficulty on-the-fly to challenge themselves and test different difficulty levels. The Power Menu can be accessed via the toggle button located under the main inventory slots, above the crafting menu. Its interface replaces the crafting menu when opened. The Power Menu can also be toggled by the Toggle Journey Powers key when the inventory is open. The Power Menu contains seven different functions: Duplication, Research, Time, Weather, Personal Power Menu, Infection Spread, and Enemy Difficulty. This menu allows the player to instantly duplicate any previously researched item indefinitely. The menu has eleven tabs, each for a different item category. The / search bar lets players search for specific items. This will search for matches in both the item name and the tooltip. If searching for an item when a category tab is open, it will only search through items in that category. The search bar also has a button at the end of it to clear any text inside it. To duplicate an item, it must first be "researched" a certain number of times, which destroys the item in the process. Using ⇧ Shift + ⚒ Use / Attack ( ) to send items to the research slot automatically researches the item. Sending an item to the player's trash slot will automatically research it once it has been overwritten by another item entering the trash slot, or immediately (without destroying it) if the number of that item placed into the trash slot is equal to or greater than the number required to unlock it for duplication. A counter of how many more of that item will be required to complete its research is displayed in the item's tooltip, or displayed above the research button when the item is placed in the slot. Research counters are tied to characters, meaning that new characters will have to research items again. The total number of researched items required varies by the item type: There are three options available within the Time Menu: The Weather Menu grants the player full control over the current weather: The Personal Power Menu includes three options related to the player: A toggle that prevents the spread of the Corruption , Crimson , and Hallow . It does not, however, reverse the spread or reset it back to their original biomes. It also does not prevent the generation of the diagonal stripes of the Hallow and Corruption/Crimson at the beginning of Hardmode when the player defeats the Wall of Flesh for the first time. A slider that sets difficulty anywhere from 0.5× to 3× the normal difficulty. 0.5× equates to the default Journey Mode difficulty, moving up through Classic Mode at 1×, Expert Mode at 2×, and Master Mode at 3×. Moving the slider enables all the changes and exclusive content obtainable in the chosen difficulty, including Expert Mode-exclusive items , Master Mode-exclusive items , increased drop chances, modified AIs , etc. The damage multiplier of town NPCs is 1× when the Enemy Difficulty Slider is lower than 2×, and 2× when the slider is 2× or higher. [ 1 ] (In non-Journey worlds, the damage multiplier is 1× / 1.5× / 1.75× .) es-formal:Modo Viaje

title: Terraria
text: The very world is at your fingertips as you fight for survival, fortune, and glory. Delve deep into cavernous expanses, seek out ever-greater foes to test your mettle in combat, or construct your own city - In the World of Terraria, the choice is yours! — terraria.org Terraria ( /təˈrɛə.ri.ə/ tə-RAIR-ee-ə [ 1 ] ) is a video game developed by Re-Logic . It was first released as a game for Windows on May 16th, 2011 but has since expanded to several other platforms. It is a 2D action-adventure , open-world sandbox / platformer game in which the player controls a single character in a generated world. Both single-player and multi-player are supported. Besides English, the in-game language can be set to German, Italian, French, Spanish, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Brazilian Portuguese, Polish, Japanese, Korean, and Traditional Chinese. The game is often compared to Minecraft by the fandom, and Terraria was partially inspired by it. [ 2 ] The gameplay has similarities; however, there are also a large number of differences, like its several bosses and events . It has been announced that a follow-up, Terraria 2 , is planned for the future. A spin-off of the game called Terraria: Otherworld was under development, but was officially canceled on April 12th, 2018. Terraria is one of the world's most sold video games , with over 64 million sold copies as of May 2025, including paid mobile downloads. [ 3 ] On January 3rd, 2022, Terraria won the "Labor of Love" Steam award . The next large content update at the time, 1.4.4 , was named after said award. Terraria is purchased via a one-time-payment. Updates are free afterward. The price differs between game versions (see below) but generally ranges between US$5–20. Various sales occasionally lower the price, to as low as US$1.99 [ 4 ] . Some platforms also offer trial/demo versions. There is also a Collector's Edition and Special Edition , which may cost slightly more, but include additional merchandise. Terraria is available on multiple PC, console, and mobile platforms, but this wiki distinguishes between the five main versions; the old generation as well as the following four versions. The Desktop version is the most extensive, featuring nearly everything ever introduced to Terraria . Traditionally being limited to Windows , the official release of Terraria on macOS and Linux platforms on August 12th, 2015 and further expanded the computer versions game from being just Windows-only. [ 5 ] The Windows version costs US$9.99 on Steam. Four historic updates of the Desktop version are permanently available on Steam: Undeluxe Edition , First Final Update , Angler's Delight , and Lunacy! . On this wiki, entries that regard content exclusive to the Desktop version will be marked with , or the banner: The Console versions of Terraria are split in terms of extensivity. The Xbox One , and the PlayStation 4 editions of the game are equivalent to Desktop 1.4.5.5 . The Nintendo Switch edition of the game is equivalent to Desktop 1.4.5.4 . The Xbox 360 , PlayStation 3 , PlayStation Vita , and Wii U editions of the game are equivalent to Desktop 1.2.4.1 , and also lack content found on the Desktop and Mobile versions, and are not planned to be updated past the 1.2.4.1 equivalent. On this wiki, the Xbox One, and Nintendo Switch editions are referred to as the "Console version" and will be marked with . (Note that the Switch version will have a separate section in the history section due to its differences with the primary console version.) The following banner may also be used: Content exclusive to the Xbox 360, , , and Wii U editions are referred to as the "Old-gen console version" and will be marked with . The following banner may also be used: The Japanese console version of Terraria is a version of the Old-gen console version with some exlusive features, available only on , , and Wii U consoles from Japanese regions. It was produced by Satoru Honma and localized by developer Spike Chunsoft, allowing for exclusive anime cross-over content to be added, including anime cameos and vanity sets. Japanese version exclusives are no longer available in the modern console version releases. On this wiki, entries that regard content exclusive to the Japanese Console version will be marked with or the banner: The Mobile version runs on Android , Amazon , iOS , and Windows Phone . It is currently equivalent to Desktop 1.4.5.5 on Amazon, Android, and iOS devices, and equivalent to Desktop 1.2.4.1 on Windows Phone devices. Like the Old-gen console version , Kindle Fire HD and prior will no longer receive updates past 1.2.4.1. Since Microsoft has completely stopped its mobile device-related business, the Windows Phone version has been completely removed from Microsoft Store. On this

title: King Slime
text: King Slime is a pre-Hardmode boss . Fighting King Slime is not required for game progression, but his defeat may reward the player with useful items. Defeating him for the first time also causes the Nerdy Slime (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) town pet to spawn from the boss. While King Slime is alive, his exclusive music King Slime will play. When Otherworldly music (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) is enabled, the track Boss 1 (Otherworldly) will play instead. King Slime can be summoned by using the Slime Crown , in any biome at any time. King Slime has a 1/300 (0.33%) chance of naturally spawning while the following conditions are met: [ 2 ] If 150 slimes are killed by players, NPCs , traps , lava , and/or summoned minions during the Slime Rain event, King Slime spawns. The following slimes will count toward this total as long as they are killed during the event: Baby Slime , Black Slime , Blue Slime , Green Slime , Jungle Slime , Pinky , Purple Slime , Red Slime , and Yellow Slime . Statue -spawned slimes are counted as well. Defeating King Slime ends the event shortly after. If all players die, King Slime despawns, and the event continues for some time before ending on its own (between 9 and 15 in-game hours). If King Slime has already been defeated in a world, it only takes 75 slime kills to spawn him. If King Slime is summoned manually with the Slime Crown and then subsequently killed, while the Slime Rain is active, the event ends in the same way as if the player killed the King Slime that naturally spawns at the end of the event. This can be quite helpful for players in later stages of the game who want to get the Slime Rain event over with as quickly as possible. In Remix worlds, killing a slime has a 1/200 (0.5%) chance of spawning King Slime, provided the boss has not yet been defeated, is not already alive, and at least one player has more than 140 maximum health and more than 8 defense . [ 3 ] Only the death of certain types of slime can trigger this: Baby Slime , Black Slime , Blue Slime , Green Slime , Jungle Slime , Pinky , Purple Slime , Red Slime , and Yellow Slime . Statue -spawned slimes are eligible as well. Slimes killed without the player dealing damage to them will not spawn King Slime. King Slime hops towards the player, attempting to deal contact damage. This behavior loop follows a set pattern: King Slime will also teleport to the player if they get too far away or if he cannot damage them (regardless of distance). King Slime becomes smaller and faster the lower his health is. He will also summon Blue Slimes (and Spiked Slimes (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) in Expert and Master Mode ) as he takes damage. King Slime is the only boss that is damaged by lava (apart from Golem in some cases).

title: Deerclops
text: In the middle of the night on snowy evenings, a terrifying roaring sound comes from the wilderness! Something is out there! — The Guide Deerclops is a pre-Hardmode boss encountered in the Snow biome . Fighting Deerclops is not required for game progression, but defeating it rewards the player with coins and other useful items. While Deerclops is alive, its exclusive music Deerclops will play. When Otherworldly music (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) is enabled, the track Boss 2 (Otherworldly) will play instead. Deerclops can be summoned manually by using a Deer Thing in the Snow or Ice biome , at any time. Deerclops can also spawn naturally at midnight during a Blizzard on the surface Snow biome when the following conditions are fulfilled: [ 1 ] If Deerclops has previously been defeated in the world, there is only a 1/4 (25%) chance for it to spawn naturally at midnight.

title: Queen Slime
text: Queen Slime is an early- Hardmode , Hallow -themed boss . Fighting Queen Slime is not required for game progression – much like her pre-Hardmode counterpart, King Slime – but her defeat may reward the player with useful items. While Queen Slime is alive, her exclusive music Queen Slime will play. When Otherworldly music (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) is enabled, the track Boss 2 (Otherworldly) will play instead. Queen Slime is summoned by using a Gelatin Crystal in The Hallow or Underground Hallow , at any time. Just like King Slime , if Queen Slime cannot reach the player, she will sink into the ground and teleport to a nearby location or directly on top of them if there are no solid blocks for her to teleport onto. Queen Slime leaps toward the player in a manner similar to her pre-Hardmode counterpart, though at a much faster speed and in bursts. After several jumps, she remains stationary for a few seconds before attacking the player in one of two ways, chosen at random or used consecutively. When Queen Slime is below 50% of her maximum health (9,000 / 14,400 / 18,360 ), she grows large wings and begins to fly above the player, passing through solid blocks. Queen Slime ceases attempting to teleport to the player and continues to use the attacks she had used during her first phase, albeit performed in a different manner while flying in midair. Upon being damaged, Queen Slime summons one of three smaller slime minions to attack the player:

title: Empress of Light
text: The Empress of Light is a Hardmode , post- Plantera boss . She is generally fought at night , and becomes " enraged " with greatly increased damage during the day. While she can be fought as soon as Plantera is defeated, she is significantly more difficult than most Hardmode bosses and will typically require post- Golem gear. The Empress of Light fight is optional and is not required for game progression, but her weapons can be greatly beneficial to players, provided they are skilled enough to take her on. While the Empress of Light is alive, her exclusive music Empress of Light will play. When Otherworldly music (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) is enabled, the track Boss 2 (Otherworldly) will play instead. The Empress of Light is summoned by killing a Prismatic Lacewing , at any time. Prismatic Lacewings spawn in The Hallow on the Surface between dusk (7:30 PM) and midnight (12:00 AM) once Plantera has been defeated. They can be caught with a Bug Net (or its upgrades), then released elsewhere and killed by the player. If released outside of the Hallow, a Prismatic Lacewing will disappear almost immediately. The Empress of Light herself does not require any bosses to be defeated prior, meaning she can be summoned earlier than her intended stage of progression, and that Prismatic Lacewings brought to a new world can be used to summon her. The Empress of Light performs a multitude of complex, projectile-focused attacks. Each attack lasts for a specific amount of time, which consists of time for performing the attack and a "wait time" after an attack. The wait time after an attack will be shortened by 0.25 seconds during her second phase, and another 0.08 seconds in Expert Mode. [ 8 ] There is also an interval between each attack, see attack pattern for details. The Empress of Light uses her attacks in a set pattern. There will be a fixed interval between each attack, which is shorter in her second phase, and is further shortened in a For the Worthy or Zenith world. When spawned, the Empress of Light will hover in place for 3 seconds [ 10 ] before executing the following attacks in order: The interval between attacks is 0.75 seconds (0.38 seconds in a For the worthy or Get fixed boi world). [ 11 ] This sequence of attacks repeats indefinitely until the second phase starts. When the Empress of Light is below half of her max health (35,000 / 49,000 / 62,475 ), she will disappear and reappear above the next estimated location of the player and enters her second phase, gaining four tendrils on her back and a brighter purple coloration (or bright pink when enraged). This transition takes a total of 3 seconds. [ 12 ] During the second phase, the Empress of Light's defense is increased to 60, and she will perform a new pattern of attacks, with slightly shorter attack intervals and higher damage: The interval between attacks is 0.33 seconds (0.17 seconds in a For the Worthy or Zenith world). [ 11 ] This sequence of attacks repeats indefinitely until the Empress of Light is defeated. The Empress of Light can be fought at any time, but she becomes "enraged" during daytime , indicated by the color of attacks turning from rainbow-colored to bright yellow. While enraged, she has a slightly different attack pattern, and all of her attacks deal ludicrously high damage [ 14 ] and will always kill the player in one hit under normal gameplay conditions. Dodge effects are ignored under certain conditions: [ 15 ] In her enraged state, her attacks will always follow her Expert Mode behavior. If all of the player's damage to the Empress of Light was dealt during daytime, she drops the Terraprisma upon being defeated.

title: Lunatic Cultist
text: The Lunatic Cultist is a Hardmode , post- Golem boss . Defeating it initiates the Lunar Events , and also causes the Clothier to begin selling the Solar and Lunar Cultist sets depending on the time of day. Unlike most bosses , the Lunatic Cultist does not drop any weapons, accessories, or materials. However, it does drop the Ancient Manipulator , a placeable crafting station used to craft many endgame items. While the Lunatic Cultist is alive, its exclusive music Lunatic Cultist will play. When Otherworldly music (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) is enabled, the track Boss 2 (Otherworldly) will play instead. The Lunatic Cultist is summoned after killing the Cultists that spawn at the Dungeon 's entrance after Skeletron and Golem have been defeated. The Lunatic Cultist makes its first brief appearance at the Mysterious Tablet after all four Cultists are killed. The Lunatic Cultist is immune to damage during its summoning animation, in which it begins to draw energy from the Mysterious Tablet as glowing particles start flowing from the tablet towards it. After this, the Mysterious Tablet breaks into pieces while the Lunatic Cultist rises into the air while its mask tilts up and shakes, appearing to laugh at the player. The Lunatic Cultist then warps to a position above where the player was after it was summoned and begins using its attacks in a set pattern. Between each attack, the Lunatic Cultist teleports to a position above the player's current location. [ 4 ] The Lunatic Cultist has the following attacks: The exact attack pattern of the Lunatic Cultist: When the Lunatic Cultist is below 50% of its maximum health (16,000 / 24,000 / 30,600 ), its defense drops to 27, and it changes its attack pattern, adding a new attack: The exact attack pattern of the Lunatic Cultist when below 50% maximum health: In Expert Mode , when the Lunatic Cultist is below 50% of its maximum health, its Fireball, Ancient Light, and Lightning Orb attacks during its attack pattern has a 1/6 (16.67%) chance to be replaced by the Ancient Doom attack: The Lunatic Cultist will only take 75% of the damage from homing projectiles. [ 14 ] The following is a list of homing projectiles that deal reduced damage to the Lunatic Cultist: Once the Lunatic Cultist is killed, the Lunar Events will be initiated with the message "Celestial creatures are invading!" Additionally, the Clothier will begin selling the Solar Cultist Clothes during the day and the Lunar Cultist Clothes at night. Every time the Lunatic Cultist is defeated, the Lunar Events will begin. During this, four celestial pillars will spawn in the world, each themed around a damage class . Defeating all four pillars will summon the final boss .

title: Dark Mage
text: I sense a kindred spirit in the Etherian Dark Mages. A pity they are our enemies, I would have liked to learn from them. — The Witch Doctor The Dark Mage is a mini-boss that spawns during the Old One's Army event , during wave 5 in Tier 1, and waves 4 and 6 in Tier 3. The Dark Mage hovers slightly above the ground and randomly chooses between three attacks:

title: Ogre
text: Have you seen an ogre yet? I want to ride on the back of one! — The Party Girl The Ogre is a Hardmode , post-one mechanical boss mini-boss that spawns in the Old One's Army event during wave 7 in Tier 2, and waves 5 and 6 in Tier 3. The Ogre moves slowly towards the Eternia Crystal and attacks it and the player in the following ways:

title: Betsy
text: Betsy is a Hardmode , post- Golem mini-boss that spawns once during the seventh and final wave of Tier 3 of the Old One's Army event . Like all enemies in the Old One's Army, she will despawn upon completion or failure of the event. Defeating Betsy is the only way to complete the final wave of the event successfully. Upon being summoned, Betsy cycles through the following attacks in the following order. If within close enough proximity to the player, she will target them instead of the Eternia Crystal .

title: Flying Dutchman
text: The Flying Dutchman is a Hardmode mini-boss that spawns during the Pirate Invasion event . The Flying Dutchman emits golden particles while hovering approximately 26 tiles above the player, and is armed with four cannons that fire explosive cannonballs toward the nearest player while occasionally spawning standard Pirate Invasion enemies that fall from the deck and onto solid ground. The boss will be defeated and drop loot upon depleting the health of all four cannons. The Flying Dutchman does not disappear after the Pirate Invasion is over (though it will explode if it has not been engaged before the invasion ends), and defeating the Flying Dutchman will not end the invasion. Several Flying Dutchmen can appear during a single Pirate Invasion, or none at all. They only appear when the event progress has passed 50%, and where there is at least 40×30 tiles space [ 1 ] .

title: Mourning Wood
text: The Mourning Wood is a Hardmode , post- Plantera mini-boss that spawns during the Pumpkin Moon event . It is the Pumpkin Moon equivalent of the Everscream , which behaves in an almost identical way to the Mourning Wood. Defeating the Mourning Wood during the Pumpkin Moon event will add 75 / 150 / 187.5 points to the final score. [ 3 ] The Mourning Wood alternates between two attacks:

title: Pumpking
text: The Pumpking can be rather two-faced, it seems. I shall not give up on peace with his spooky people! — The Princess The Pumpking is a Hardmode , post- Plantera mini-boss that spawns during the Pumpkin Moon event . He is the event's final enemy and can move freely through blocks . He is the equivalent to the Ice Queen from the Frost Moon , being the Pumpkin Moon's most powerful enemy, coming in at the last waves of the event, and being the event's only flying boss. Defeating the Pumpking during the Pumpkin Moon event will add 150 / 300 / 375 points to the final score, giving the most number of points for any enemy in the event. [ 4 ] Like the Mourning Wood , the later the Pumpkin Moon wave in which the Pumpking is killed in, the more likely he is to drop items. A Pumpking defeated during wave 15 has a guaranteed chance to drop at least one item from his standard drop table. The Pumpking has two arms, called Pumpking Blade s, which behave much like Skeletron 's hands by swiping towards the player to inflict contact damage, but unlike Skeletron's hands, the Pumpking Blades are entirely immune to damage . The Pumpking repeatedly switches between three different attack patterns at random, with his current attack mode indicated by his facial expression. When transitioning to his next mode, his head rotates to reveal a different expression.

title: Ice Queen
text: Diplomacy did not work so well with the Ice Queen, I'm afraid. She's rather cold-hearted! He he! — The Princess The Ice Queen is a Hardmode , post- Plantera mini-boss that spawns during the Frost Moon event . She is the event's final enemy and can move freely through blocks . She is the equivalent to the Pumpking from the Pumpkin Moon , being the Frost Moon's most powerful enemy, coming in at the last waves of the event, and being the event's only flying boss. Defeating the Ice Queen during the Frost Moon event will add 150 / 300 / 375 points to the final score, giving the most number of points for any enemy in the event. [ 3 ] The Ice Queen's behavior is characterized by astonishingly fast movement and evasive maneuvers while constantly firing off ranged attacks aimed toward the player. She will hover and attempt to position herself above the player while randomly cycling through the following attacks: [ 1 ] As the Ice Queen's health is depleted, her acceleration and maximum horizontal speed increase. Additionally, the firing rates of her attacks increase, as detailed in the following table. Note that these changes happen as the Ice Queen's health passes the listed thresholds, and are not continuous; thus, for example, her frost wave firing rate will be 2.07 waves/second at 100% health and also at 75% health, but as soon as her health drops below 75%, the rate of fire will increase to 2.22/sec.

title: Santa-NK1
text: Santa-NK1 is a Hardmode , post- Plantera mini-boss that spawns during the Frost Moon event . When below half health, the face and beard of the Santa mask are torn apart to reveal a rather morbid mechanical skull underneath. Defeating Santa-NK1 during the Frost Moon event will add 100 / 200 / 250 points to the final score. [ 3 ] Santa-NK1 has four different attacks, all of which occur independently of each other: [ 1 ] All of Santa-NK1's attacks either increase their rate of fire or increase their chance of occurring as its health drops, as described above; the specific effects are listed in the following table. Note that these changes happen as Santa-NK1's health passes the listed thresholds, and are not continuous; thus, for example, its chain gun firing rate will be 3.75 bullets/second at 100% health and also at 75% health, but as soon as its health drops below 75%, the rate of fire will increase to 4.29/sec.

title: Everscream
text: The Everscream is a Hardmode , post- Plantera mini-boss that spawns during the Frost Moon event . It is the Frost Moon equivalent of the Mourning Wood , which behaves in an almost identical way to the Everscream. Defeating the Everscream during the Frost Moon event will add 50 / 100 / 125 points to the final score. [ 3 ] The Everscream alternates between two attacks:

title: Guide
text: I am here to give you advice on what to do next. It is recommended that you talk with me anytime you get stuck. — The Guide The Guide is usually the first NPC a player encounters. He spawns near the player when the world is created. He provides tips mainly aimed at new players via the " Help " option and displays crafting recipes (including the crafting station required) of items shown to him via the " Crafting " option. Unlike most other NPCs, the Guide spawns upon world creation, before a house is available. He will, however, move into the first house built. If he is killed, a proper house will need to be built before he can respawn, but he has no additional requirements. The Guide will defend himself against nearby enemies using a Wooden Bow , firing Wooden Arrows initially and then upgrading to Flaming Arrows during Hardmode . He deals 8 / 12 / 14 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. [ 1 ] For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Guide will transform into his Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. He can be returned to his classic look by submerging him in Shimmer again. The Guide may have any of the following names: The Guide displays one of the lines listed below when his " Help " option is selected. He cycles through the lines when the option is selected repeatedly. Much of his advice text depends on the player's current game progression. title: Nurse
text: You look half digested. Have you been chasing slimes again? — The Nurse The Nurse is an NPC who will move in once the following criteria have been met: The Nurse will fully restore the player's health and cure them of any active debuffs at the cost of coins (there are exceptions, see Notes and History ). Healing costs are 1 CC per health restored, plus an additional 1 SC per active debuff on the player, all multiplied by the Nurse's current Happiness price adjustment , and then doubled if playing on Expert Mode . Also, the Nurse charges increasing amounts of money for healing as the game progresses; these specific increases are listed below . Thus, at the start of the game, the cost of restoring 100 health can range from 75 CC / 1 SC 50 CC (with maximum happiness) to 1 SC 50 CC / 3 SC (with maximum unhappiness), while by endgame it can range from 1 GC 50 SC / 3 GC up to 3 GC / 6 GC . The Nurse will defend herself against nearby enemies by throwing green syringes that damage and poison enemies for 3–5 seconds. She can also throw red syringes to heal herself and other nearby NPCs for 20 health, which can also home in on targets. [ 2 ] She deals 8 / 12 / 14 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. [ 1 ] The cost of healing changes based on three factors: The Nurse's current Happiness , game progression, and Expert Mode . Happiness affects the Nurse's price by raising or lowering it to within a range of 75% to 150% of its base (in the same way that other NPCs' prices are affected by their happiness). Playing in Expert (or Master ) mode further affects prices by permanently doubling them. Progression-based price increases are based on which bosses have been defeated in the current world. These increases are listed in the table below; only the single highest applicable increase is chosen and applied. For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Nurse will transform into her Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. She can be returned to her classic look by submerging her in Shimmer again. title: Dryad
text: Be safe; Terraria needs you! — The Dryad The Dryad is an NPC vendor who sells various nature-themed items, including seeds , powders , and Planter Boxes . She will move in once the following criteria have been met: In addition to selling items, the Dryad can also report the percentages of Corruption / Crimson and Hallow that exist in the current world, which is based on the total number of blocks of each corresponding biome. The Dryad will defend herself against nearby enemies by casting the Dryad's Ward, which summons two rotating circles of leaves around her, with the outer circle indicating the range of the attack. The attack lasts 10 seconds in total [ 2 ] , however, if the Dryad takes damage when casting, the attack immediately ends. Counting from when the circles of leaves are summoned (and assuming the Dryad is uninterrupted), within the first 1.67 seconds, the radius of the Dryad's Ward is 18.75 tiles (300 pixels). The radius then generally increases to 37.5 tiles (600 pixels) over the course of 3.33 seconds, remains unchanged for another 3.33 seconds, after which the radius generally increases to 75 tiles (1200 pixels) over the course of 1.17 seconds before finally dissipating. 0.5 seconds after the circles of leaves are summoned [ 3 ] , the Dryad's Blessing buff is granted to players and other town NPCs within its radius, while Dryad's Bane is inflicted on enemies with a clear line of sight inside its radius. Dryad's Blessing provides defense , health regeneration , and a thorns effect (similar to the Thorns Potion ), while Dryad's Bane deals damage over time. See Dryad's Blessing page for details about the buff and debuff. The Dryad will report the current percentage of Corruption / Crimson / Hallow coverage in the world with the " Status " option. The Dryad makes the distinction between Corruption and Crimson if they are present in the same world, and will list their percentages separately, though they both contribute to the overall total evil tile percentage in her final report. [ 4 ] The percentages of the respective biome is calculated by related tile count, with the following formulas: See below for a list of counted tiles. If the world has no Corruption, Crimson, or Hallow, the And Good Riddance! achievement will be granted upon choosing the option. The following tiles will count towards the total tile count. [ 6 ] When the player has a Joja Cola in their inventory , the Dryad will have special quotes hinting to the player to hold the Joja Cola. When the player is holding a Joja Cola, the " Status " option will be replaced with " Purify ". After choosing the option, the Joja Cola will be consumed as an animation of a Junimo jumping out of a star-shaped portal plays out. The Junimo then pulls out a Stardrop that the player can collect before disappearing. For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Dryad will transform into her Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. She can be returned to her classic look by submerging her in Shimmer again. The Dryad may have any of the following names: A world with both Celebration Mk 10 and Purify this enabled will spawn a shimmered Dryad named Lilith at game start. This is not a possible name for the Dryad under any other circumstances. title: Goblin Tinkerer
text: To be honest, most goblins aren't exactly rocket scientists. Well, some are. — The Goblin Tinkerer The Goblin Tinkerer is an NPC vendor who sells several unique items and also provides the reforge function. He will move in once the following criteria have been met: The Goblin Tinkerer's reforge option allows the player to randomize the modifier of a weapon or accessory , which makes the resulting item's stats better or worse than they were originally. Reforging costs one-third of the item's current value , which can also be affected by its current modifier. The reforge cost is also affected by the Goblin Tinkerer's happiness , and therefore can be decreased by equipping a Discount Card or Greedy Ring . [ 2 ] The Goblin Tinkerer will defend himself against nearby enemies by throwing Spiky Balls . He deals 15 / 22 / 26 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. title: Clothier
text: Thanks again for freeing me from my curse. Felt like something jumped up and bit me. — The Clothier The Clothier is an NPC vendor who mainly sells vanity items . He will move in once the following criteria have been met: He is the same character as the Old Man who guards the Dungeon before Skeletron is defeated. After defeating Skeletron, the Old Man is freed from his curse, and he disappears from the Dungeon's entrance. His clothes are no longer tattered, his eyes have turned from red to blue, and he is wearing a Red Hat and scarf. Equipping the Clothier Voodoo Doll allows the player to attack and kill the Clothier (similar to the Guide Voodoo Doll ) with any damage-inflicting item. Doing so at night re-summons Skeletron, but killing the Clothier without the Clothier Voodoo Doll equipped (such as by using the Flymeal on its own) will not summon the boss. The Clothier will defend himself against nearby enemies by firing shadowflame skull projectiles that damage and inflict the Shadowflame debuff on enemies for 5–10 seconds. [ 2 ] He deals 16 / 24 / 28 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. [ 1 ] For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Clothier will transform into his Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. He can be returned to his classic look by submerging him in Shimmer again. title: Tax Collector
text: Death and taxes, nothing more sure in life than that! — The Tax Collector The Tax Collector is a Hardmode NPC who taxes other housed NPCs to earn coins for players. He will move in once the following criteria have been met: When the Tax Collector is present, 50 CC will be earned per real-world minute (each in-game hour), for each NPC that has a house (including the Tax Collector himself but excluding the Old Man , Traveling Merchant , Skeleton Merchant , and town pets ). The Tax Collector stores these funds, up to 25 GC . These stats are doubled in Celebration Mk 10 and Zenith worlds (i.e. 1 SC per NPC and 50 GC maximum). [ 2 ] Speaking to him will reveal the amount stored thus far, and offer the " Collect " option to collect the funds. His tax limit (as well as his current funds and how much he collects per minute) varies depending on his current happiness ; thus, how much he earns per minute will be between 33 CC and 66 CC , while his limit will be between 16 GC 66 SC 66 CC and 33 GC 33 SC 33 CC . [ 3 ] Unlike other town NPCs, the Tax Collector is immune to lava , due to being spawned from a Tortured Soul. [ 4 ] The Tax Collector will attack nearby enemies with his Classy Cane , for which he has a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of dropping upon death. For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Tax Collector will transform into his Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. He can be returned to his classic look by submerging him in Shimmer again. title: Tavernkeep
text: This is a big upgrade from wiping that table all day. — The Tavernkeep The Tavernkeep is a unique NPC vendor who only accepts Defender Medals as currency for most items instead of coins . He will move in once the following criteria have been met: The Tavernkeep sells sentry summon weapons which are associated with the Old One's Army event and can only be used within that event at first. They function elsewhere only after the event is completed once successfully. Each of the Tavernkeep's sentry summon weapons come in three tiers: rod, cane, and staff, with the staff being the most expensive and the most powerful. The armor he sells also associates each sentry type with a specific class: Ballista for melee or tank-style users, Flameburst for magic users, Explosive Trap for rangers , and Lightning Aura for "speedy melee" (or summoners ). When the player selects the " Eternia Crystal " option for the first time, the Tavernkeep will give the player 10 Defender Medals for free. All future Defender Medals must be earned by either defeating the Old One's Army outright or by clearing its later waves. The Tavernkeep will defend himself against nearby enemies by throwing mugs of Ale . He deals 24 / 36 / 42 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. [ 1 ] The Tavernkeep also has a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of dropping the Ale Tosser upon death. The Unconscious Man is one of the few entities that can be detected by the Lifeform Analyzer or its upgrades. Note: While the prices of the first five listed items (those bought by coins ) can be decreased by equipping a Discount Card or Greedy Ring , and are affected by the NPC's happiness level , the rest of the stock always costs a fixed amount of Defender Medals . For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Tavernkeep will transform into his Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. He can be returned to his classic look by submerging him in Shimmer again. The Tavernkeep may have any of the following names: These quotes appear when the " Eternia Crystal " option is selected. title: Stylist
text: For you I think we'll do something... low maintenance. — The Stylist The Stylist is an NPC vendor who can change the player's hairstyle and hair color. She also sells a variety of Hair Dyes , which apply dynamic effects to the player's hair. She will move in once the following criteria have been met: Selecting the " Hair Style " option in the Stylist's dialog opens a window where the player can choose a new hairstyle and color. The changes are immediately visible on the character for preview. Permanently applying the changes incurs a fee: switching to a different hairstyle costs 10 GC ; changing hair color costs 2 GC . Hairstyle and color can be changed repeatedly and at any time. Like with all other vendors, the Stylist's prices can vary depending on her happiness ; this also affects her prices for hairstyle and color changes, as follows: The combined cost of changing hairstyle and color is multiplied by her happiness-based price multiplier, and then rounded to the nearest multiple of 1 GC . [ 2 ] Thus, the cost of changing hairstyles can be 8–15 , changing hair colors can cost 2–3 , and changing both hairstyle and hair color at once can range from 10 GC to 18 GC . The Stylist will defend herself against nearby enemies with her Stylish Scissors . She deals 10 / 15 / 17 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. [ 1 ] The Stylist also has a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of dropping the Stylish Scissors upon death. The Webbed Stylist is one of the few entities that can be detected by the Lifeform Analyzer or its upgrades. For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Stylist will transform into her Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. She can be returned to her classic look by submerging her in Shimmer again. title: Angler
text: Psst! I might have a job for you. Don't think you can say no, either! — The Angler The Angler is a unique NPC concerned exclusively with fishing . He does not sell anything, but rather assigns quests to players. Quest objectives generally involve catching him rare fish from particular biomes or layers , for which he offers various rewards. He will move in once the following criteria have been met: The Angler will defend himself against nearby enemies by throwing Frost Daggerfish . He deals 10 / 15 / 17 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. [ 1 ] The Sleeping Angler is one of the few entities that can be detected by the Lifeform Analyzer (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) or its upgrades. After a quest has been completed, the Angler will reward the player with one item, a number of coins , and possibly some bait . Notes: Only one of the following special rewards may be obtained. The chances listed are affected by the number of quests completed and the Angler's happiness. Chances are rolled in the order given by the list below, starting from the top. This means rewards near the bottom have a slightly lowered chance of being obtained than what is listed. These chances are not affected by luck . The player is also sometimes rewarded with a random piece of furniture from the list below, similarly to the main pool. The furniture is guaranteed after having completed at least 100 quests (100th quest included). If bait is being rewarded, the chances of getting which type are in the order listed below. The chances listed are affected by the number of quests completed and the Angler's happiness. As noted above, the chance of obtaining any bait at all ranges from 50% to 100% (at base happiness). The amount of bait obtained is determined randomly, with a minimum of 1 and maximum of 7 and with the average amount depending on to the number of quests completed. Six independent rolls are performed, with chances N-in-25, N-in-50, N-in-100, N-in-150, N-in-200, and N-in-250 (where N is the number of quests completed plus one ), and those rolls which succeed increase the amount of bait by 1 each. Thus, the amount will usually vary randomly between 1 and 7 bait, until maxing out at the 249th quest. [ 3 ] A few examples of average bait amounts for different quest completion totals are as follows: For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Angler will transform into his Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. He can be returned to his classic look by submerging him in Shimmer again. title: Party Girl
text: Do you party? Sometimes? Hm, okay then we can talk... — The Party Girl The Party Girl is an NPC vendor who sells festive novelty items and furniture . Unlike other town NPCs, the Party Girl only has a 1/40 (2.5%) chance to be eligible for spawning every 2-minute interval, during which she is subject to regular NPC spawn rate, once the criteria below are met. In other words, she will take on average 40 times longer to spawn than regular NPCs. Along with this percentage chance, she will move in once the following criteria are met: If the Party Girl is killed, she will respawn with normal spawn chances instead of being rarer. The Party Girl will defend herself against nearby enemies by throwing Happy Grenades . She deals 30 / 45 / 52 damage at the start of the game, and gains various stat boosts as the player progresses. [ 1 ] The Party Girl also has a 1/4 (25%) chance of dropping 30–60 Happy Grenades upon death. During a natural Party , she will give a Slice of Cake to the first player that talks to her. Entering a drunk world for the first time permanently unlocks a " Music " option in the Party Girl's dialogue window, allowing the player to toggle the game's default soundtrack for the Terraria: Otherworld soundtrack. Once unlocked, the option to toggle the music will be permanently available across all players and all worlds. Every song in Terraria's soundtrack is replaced. Songs that do not have a Terraria: Otherworld equivalent will use the most relevant song; for example, the Underground Jungle will simply use Terraria: Otherworld's Jungle theme, rather than Terraria's Underground Jungle theme. Every new song has its own unique Music Box , all of which are called "Otherworldly Music Boxes". In a multiplayer world, players only hear the altered soundtrack if they have used the " Music " option themselves, which is only present if they have unlocked it before by visiting a drunk world. However, all players can listen to Otherworldly Music Boxes in multiplayer worlds, regardless of whether they have toggled or unlocked the option before. Upon leaving the world, the soundtrack will revert to the default. For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Party Girl will transform into her Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. She can be returned to her classic look by submerging her in Shimmer again. title: Truffle
text: There's been such a huge rumor that's being spread about me, 'If you can't beat him, eat him!' — The Truffle The Truffle is a Hardmode NPC vendor who sells various mushroom -themed items. He can arrive homelessly as soon as Hardmode starts, and he can only move into vacant houses that is located in (or near) a surface Glowing Mushroom biome (i.e. one that is above 0 elevation ). If the Truffle spawns into a vacant house in a surface Glowing Mushroom biome, he is considered unlocked and can then be reassigned to a house at any elevation, as long as it is in a Glowing Mushroom biome. Moving a homeless Truffle into suitable house is not considered unlocking him; in this case, he can only be reassigned into houses in surface Glowing Mushroom biomes. [ 2 ] After the Truffle dies, if the Truffle is unlocked, he can respawn into vacant houses located in Glowing Mushroom biomes at any height; if he is not unlocked, he can only respawn into vacant houses located in surface Glowing Mushroom biomes. Otherwise, he can only respawn homelessly. When threatened by enemies, the Truffle will release Mushroom Spores, which hover in midair and explode into spore clouds upon contact with enemies. For more information, see NPC happiness . When submerged in Shimmer , the Truffle will transform into his Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. He can be returned to his classic look by submerging him in Shimmer again. title: Princess
text: I really appreciate all the good you've done for this land! Thank you! — The Princess The Princess is a Hardmode , post- Plantera NPC vendor who sells royal-themed vanity items and furniture , and is typically the final town NPC that the player encounters. She will move in once the following criteria have been met: [ 1 ] These requirements only apply to the initial spawning. If the Princess is killed, she will automatically respawn after some time. The Princess will attack nearby enemies with the Resonance Scepter , for which she has a 1/8 (12.5%) chance of dropping upon death if the current world is in Hardmode . The Princess is unique in that she does not have any biome or neighbor preferences. She loves up to three NPCs that live nearby and is liked by all NPCs around her. She also hates being lonely and her unhappiness is maxed to 150% if she is not surrounded by at least two other NPCs within 25 tiles, or two other NPCs that are between 25 and 120 tiles away. [ 2 ] When submerged in Shimmer , the Princess will transform into her Shimmered form. This has no impact on gameplay or dialogue and is only a visual change. She can be returned to her classic look by submerging her in Shimmer again.
